name: Mary (Mother of Jesus)
shortDescription: Mary is revered in Christianity as the mother of Jesus Christ. Her acceptance of God's will and her role in the Incarnation are central to Christian theology.
description: Mary, a Jewish woman of Nazareth in Galilee, is a pivotal figure in Christianity, primarily revered as the mother of Jesus Christ. According to the Gospels, she was betrothed to Joseph when the angel Gabriel appeared to her, announcing that she would miraculously conceive and bear a son to be named Jesus, the Son of God. Mary's acceptance of this divine calling, known as the Annunciation, is considered a model of faith and obedience. She journeyed to Bethlehem with Joseph, where Jesus was born. Mary witnessed Jesus's life, ministry, crucifixion, and resurrection. She is often depicted as present at key events, such as the Wedding at Cana and the foot of the Cross. In Christian theology, Mary is venerated for her purity, humility, and role in salvation history. She is believed to have been assumed into heaven, body and soul, and is often invoked as an intercessor. Her title 'Theotokos' (God-bearer) emphasizes her role in the Incarnation. She symbolizes motherhood, compassion, and unwavering faith and is a central figure in Christian art, literature, and devotion.
